_**User (2026-04-23 14:08:34Z)**_

good morning - new day! after completing a scan, we are going to need to assign an x,y,z location that will represent the location of the celestial body in relation to the solar system.  it is intended that we should be able to navigate through the solar system in detail and view various celestial bodies, both very large (Jupiter) and small - asteroids.  the existing kinematics structure seems to be a good sibling to this concept, and maybe a good place to start to look to add the new properties.  so, two first steps - create a new celestial-body location structure and assign a set of random locations for the asteroids viewable in the cold-boot-scan scene.  understand that these locations will need to be more or less close to each other as they will represent created celestial bodies in our solar system.

_**User (2026-04-23 14:27:23Z)**_

add a new request response message used in our socket service to upsert a celestial body.  A celestial body will be an object that will reside in a solar system, and have kinematics, composition (material), and have unique identifiers.  This request will be sent after a successful scan.  it will include created and updated dates as per our standard, but also a character createdBy id.  it will not be associated with a player.  A celestial body will have a Solar System reference for ease of filtering, and we will assume that the Sol solar system is the parent reference for the moment.

_**User (2026-04-23 15:21:37Z)**_

planning to add kinematics and location information for the drone that is generated when we create a character.  expect that the initial drone will be in the Sol SolarSystem, somewhere in the asteroid belt region.  use this drone location in the cold-boot-scan creation of the initial set of random asteroids.  what other questions do you have before implementing?

---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

Great direction. Before implementing, these are the key decisions I need from you:

1. Source of truth for spawn position:
Should the initial drone position be generated client-side when character is created, or should we send a create request and let backend assign the canonical position/kinematics?

2. Coordinate model:
Do you want drone location to use the same structure as celestial bodies (positionKm under a location object), or keep using the existing drone kinematics position/reference shape already used in [src/app/model/drone-list.ts](src/app/model/drone-list.ts)?

3. Asteroid belt spawn envelope:
What belt bounds should I use for initial drone spawn in Sol?
I can default to the same approximate range we used for asteroid cluster centers (2.2 AU to 3.2 AU equivalent in km).

4. Initial velocity profile:
Should the starter drone have:
- a realistic orbital-ish tangential velocity in km/s, or
- a near-stationary velocity for gameplay simplicity at first launch?

5. Reference metadata:
Confirm desired reference values for drone kinematics:
- solarSystemId: sol
- referenceKind: barycentric
- distanceUnit: km
- velocityUnit: km/s
- epochMs: now
If different, tell me the exact values.

6. Character creation contract change:
Should the character creation response include the newly created starter drone (with location/kinematics), or should client make a follow-up drone-list request to retrieve it?

7. Cold-boot asteroid seeding anchor:
For [src/app/scene/cold-boot-scan.ts](src/app/scene/cold-boot-scan.ts), do you want asteroid cluster center:
- exactly centered on drone position, or
- offset from drone position by a configurable distance so the pilot has to travel/scan nearby?

8. Determinism:
Should starter drone spawn be deterministic per character (for example seeded by characterId), or random every new character creation?

9. Backward compatibility:
Do we need to support older backend payloads that do not yet include drone kinematics/location during rollout, or can we require the new fields immediately?

10. Persistence ownership:
Should starter drone record include createdByCharacterId similar to celestial bodies, or continue existing player+character ownership model used by drone list requests?

If you want, I can proceed immediately using sensible defaults for all 10 and implement end-to-end in one pass.

---
